title: StealthTalk Anomalous Authentication
descriptionMarkdown:
The StealthTalk Anomalous Authentication connector ingests four classes of anomalous user-authentication events from the StealthTalk Enterprise messenger into a custom Log Analytics table via the Logs Ingestion API.

Events:
- **OffHoursLogin** - login outside the user's configured working hours.
- **NewDeviceLogin** - login from a previously-unseen device.
- **GeoAnomalyLogin** - login from a country/city that does not match the user's assigned geo zone.
- **MultiFailLogin** - multiple consecutive failed authentication attempts.

sampleQueries.description: All anomalous-auth events in the last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Top users by event count (last 7 days)
sampleQueries.query:
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize EventCount = count() by UserId
| top 20 by EventCount desc

sampleQueries.description: StealthTalk events visible through the standard ASIM Authentication union
sampleQueries.query:
imAuthentication
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where EventVendor == "StealthTalk"

dataTypes.name: StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

value:
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| summarize LastEvent = max(TimeGenerated)
| project IsConnected = LastEvent > ago(7d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions are required.

read permissions to shared keys for the workspace are required. [See the documentation to learn more about workspace keys](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/agent-windows#obtain-workspace-id-and-key).

customs.name: StealthTalk Enterprise instance
customs.description: A deployed StealthTalk Enterprise tenant with admin access to configure the outgoing webhook to the Log Analytics workspace.

customs.name: Microsoft Entra App Registration
customs.description: A service principal that StealthTalk uses to authenticate to the Logs Ingestion API. Must have the **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role on the deployed Data Collection Rule.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 1 - Deploy the ingestion infrastructure
instructionSteps.description: Deploy the custom log table, Data Collection Endpoint and Data Collection Rule into your workspace's resource group using the included ARM template `Data Connectors/ARM/StealthTalk_Ingestion.json`. The template parameters allow you to rename the table (default `StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL`), customise the DCE/DCR names, and set the table retention.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 2 - Deploy the ASIM Authentication parsers
instructionSteps.description:
Two parser ARM templates are shipped under `Parsers/`:

1. `Parsers/vimASimAuthenticationStealthTalk.json` - installs `vimAuthenticationStealthTalk` (filtering parser, ASIM Authentication 0.1.3 contract) and `ASimAuthenticationStealthTalk` (non-filtering wrapper).
2. `Parsers/imAuthentication_extension.json` - overrides the workspace `imAuthentication()` union function so StealthTalk events are returned alongside Microsoft built-in sources. **Prerequisite:** Microsoft's `FullDeploymentAuthentication.json` ASIM parser pack must already be deployed in the workspace - install it from the Azure-Sentinel GitHub repository first if it is not.

Deploy both via Azure Portal -> 'Deploy a custom template' -> 'Build your own template in the editor' -> 'Load file', supplying your workspace name as the parameter.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 3 - Capture the DCE endpoint and DCR Immutable ID
instructionSteps.description: Open the Data Collection Endpoint and Data Collection Rule resources in the same resource group. From the DCE properties, copy the **Logs Ingestion endpoint URI**. From the DCR Overview, copy the **Immutable ID** (e.g. `dcr-...`). You will paste both into the StealthTalk admin console.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 4 - Create a Microsoft Entra App Registration for StealthTalk
instructionSteps.description: Create a new App Registration in your Microsoft Entra tenant. Generate a client secret (or use a certificate). Note the **Tenant ID**, **App (client) ID** and **Client secret**.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 5 - Grant the App Registration the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on the DCR
instructionSteps.description: Open the Data Collection Rule -> Access control (IAM) -> Add role assignment -> Role: **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** -> Assign access to: User, group, or service principal -> select the App Registration created in Step 4 -> Review + assign.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 6 - Configure StealthTalk to send events
instructionSteps.description:
In the StealthTalk admin console, configure the Microsoft Sentinel/Log Analytics integration with the values captured in Steps 3 and 4:

- **DCE endpoint** - the Logs Ingestion endpoint URI from Step 3.
- **DCR Immutable ID** - from Step 3.
- **Stream name** - `Custom-StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL`.
- **Tenant ID / App ID / Client secret** - from Step 4.

Save and trigger one or more anomalous events (use the included simulator scripts under `assets/Scripts/`) to validate the pipeline.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 7 - Verify ingestion and ASIM normalisation
instructionSteps.description:
Run the validation queries (see the **README** or the **Validation** sample queries above). All three queries must return matching counts within a few minutes of triggering events:

- Raw `StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL` count > 0.
- `vimAuthenticationStealthTalk` count > 0.
- `imAuthentication | where EventVendor == "StealthTalk"` count > 0.

If the third query returns zero, the `imAuthentication` extension parser was not deployed correctly - redeploy `Parsers/imAuthentication_extension.json` from Step 2.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 8 - Enable analytic rules, hunting queries, workbook, and playbook
instructionSteps.description: Under **Content management -> Content hub -> StealthTalk -> Manage**, enable the four Analytic Rule templates, the three Hunting Query templates, the Workbook, and the Teams Playbook. For the Playbook, supply the Teams incoming-webhook URL of your channel as a deployment parameter, and create an Automation Rule to run the Playbook on incidents from any StealthTalk analytic rule.
